sprints.title: Schema and hand-written designs
sprints.goal: Define the data formats and prove you can describe a Lego design as JSON.

tasks.text: Define `core/schema/library.schema.json` -- piece library format (piece ID, dimensions, connection points, LDraw reference, colors)
Define `core/schema/design.schema.json` -- design document format (meta, pieces with position/rotation/connections, groups)
Write 2-3 hand-crafted design JSON files as test fixtures in `tests/fixtures/`: single brick on baseplate, 2x4 wall (4 bricks stacked), L-shaped structure
Add screw definitions to the wood_basic library and library schema: screw types with length, diameter. Update design schema so connections can reference a `fastener` field
Write `core/library.py` -- load a library JSON, look up pieces by ID

sprints.done_when: You can write a design JSON by hand and validate it against the schema. Test fixtures exist as ground truth for later sprints.

sprints.title: Piece library from LDraw
sprints.goal: Populate the Lego piece library with real parts and parse LDraw geometry.

tasks.text: Create `libraries/lego_basic/library.json` with ~20 piece types (bricks, plates, baseplates) mapped to LDraw part IDs
Define connection points for each piece (studs on top, anti-studs on bottom, count matching stud grid)
Download LDraw parts library subset -- `.dat` files for the pieces we use into `libraries/lego_basic/parts/`
Extend `core/library.py` to parse LDraw `.dat` files into Trimesh geometry

sprints.done_when: `library.load("lego_basic")` returns a catalog. `library.get_geometry("brick_2x4")` returns a Trimesh mesh inspectable in a Python REPL.

sprints.title: Validator
sprints.goal: Given a design JSON and a piece library, report all errors.

tasks.text: Write `core/validator.py` -- takes a design dict and library, returns a validation report (errors and warnings referencing piece IDs)
Connection validity: check compatible point types and position alignment within tolerance
Collision detection: 3D occupancy grid at stud-pitch resolution, rasterize bounding volumes, flag overlaps
Screw collision detection (wood only): check screws entering a post from opposite faces don't overlap inside the piece
Write `core/stability/base.py` -- stability checker interface (`check(design) -> list[piece_id]`)
Write `core/stability/lego.py` -- graph reachability from ground via BFS from baseplates
Completeness check: detect disconnected subgraphs (single connected component per object)
Test against Sprint 1 fixtures + intentionally broken designs in `tests/test_validator.py`

sprints.done_when: `validator.validate(design, library)` returns a structured report. Broken designs produce specific error messages. All tests pass.

sprints.title: Offscreen renderer
sprints.goal: Produce multi-angle PNG images from a design JSON.

tasks.text: Write `core/renderer.py` -- takes design dict and library, produces PNG images
Load piece geometry from library (Trimesh meshes), place at design position/rotation with color materials
Render from 6 fixed camera angles: front, back, left, right, top, isometric
Test with hand-crafted fixtures -- visually confirm rendered images in `tests/test_renderer.py`

sprints.done_when: `renderer.render(design, library, output_dir)` writes 6 PNGs showing correct Lego structures from multiple angles.

sprints.title: CLI skeleton and manual design loop
sprints.goal: A terminal interface where you can load, validate, and render designs interactively.

tasks.text: Write `cli/main.py` -- entry point, argument parsing (project directory, library selection)
Write `cli/display.py` -- Rich-formatted output for validation reports, piece lists, design summaries
Implement commands: `load`, `validate`, `render`, `export`, `quit`
Add LDraw `.ldr` export in `core/exporter.py` -- convert design JSON to LDraw format

sprints.done_when: Run `python -m cli`, load a hand-written design, see colored validation results, view rendered images. Manual edit-reload-validate-render loop works.

sprints.title: AI agent loop
sprints.goal: An LLM can generate and iterate on designs given a text description.

tasks.text: Write `core/agent.py` -- the design agent loop with system prompt construction (library docs, schema, design state, validation report, examples)
Incremental building: agent adds bricks iteratively, validator runs after each addition, errors fed back immediately
Visual self-evaluation: renderer produces screenshots fed back to the agent for iteration
Convergence logic: stop after N iterations or when agent declares complete
Model-agnostic LLM interface (any provider accepting messages + images)
Test with simple prompt ("a 4x4 tower, 6 bricks tall") in `tests/test_agent.py`

sprints.done_when: `agent.design("a small table", library)` runs the full loop and returns a validated design JSON with inspectable intermediate steps.

sprints.title: Instruction generator
sprints.goal: Given a completed design, produce step-by-step assembly instructions with rendered images.

tasks.text: Write `core/instructor.py` -- takes design dict, returns ordered list of build steps
Bottom-up ordering (lowest z first), respect subassembly groups, 1-3 pieces per step
Verify every intermediate state is stable using the stability checker
Output per step: piece IDs, instruction text, camera angle suggestion
Render each step: highlight new pieces, mute previous pieces
Test in `tests/test_instructor.py`

sprints.done_when: `instructor.generate_steps(design, library)` returns steps with rendered images showing build progress you can follow.

sprints.title: Full CLI integration
sprints.goal: Complete terminal experience -- describe a design in natural language, get validated design with rendered images and assembly instructions.

tasks.text: Add chat mode to CLI in `cli/chat.py` -- interactive back-and-forth with the AI agent
Wire agent loop into CLI: show progress (current step, validation status, brick count)
Auto-run instruction generator after design completion, display steps in terminal
Instruction step-through: `next`/`prev`/`goto N` commands with rendered images and text
LLM-generated instruction text replacing template text from Sprint 7
Project persistence: save design JSON, rendered images, instruction steps, and LDraw export to project directory

sprints.done_when: Run `python -m cli`, type "a small bench", watch AI build it, step through assembly instructions with images, export as `.ldr`.

sprints.title: 3D viewport
sprints.goal: Render a design JSON as an interactive 3D scene in the browser.

tasks.text: Set up React + Three.js (React Three Fiber) frontend project
Write `web/frontend/src/viewport/Scene.jsx` -- load design JSON, place piece geometries with correct colors
Write `web/frontend/src/viewport/PieceRenderer.jsx` -- load LDraw geometry as glTF/Three.js format, apply materials
Orbit controls, zoom, pan. Click-to-select pieces with info display

sprints.done_when: Open `localhost:3000`, see a Lego design you can rotate, zoom, and click on. Design JSON loaded as a static file.

sprints.title: API server and chat panel
sprints.goal: Connect the web frontend to the core backend via an API.

tasks.text: Write `web/backend/api.py` -- FastAPI server wrapping core functions (validate, render, agent, instructions)
WebSocket endpoint for streaming agent progress (incremental steps pushed to client)
Write `web/backend/session.py` -- manage design sessions (state, conversation history)
Write `web/frontend/src/panels/ChatPanel.jsx` -- text input, message history, agent responses
Connect chat panel to 3D viewport: agent updates trigger viewport re-render

sprints.done_when: Open web app, type a description in chat, watch the 3D viewport update as the AI builds the design brick by brick.

sprints.title: IDE layout and instruction mode
sprints.goal: The full IDE experience with all three panels and instruction step-through.

tasks.text: Write `web/frontend/src/panels/LibraryPanel.jsx` -- piece library with thumbnails, current parts list with quantities and colors
Implement instruction mode in viewport: step-through controls, highlight new pieces, mute previous, overlay text
Wire instruction generator output to instruction mode UI
Split-panel layout with resizable panels (left library, center viewport, right chat)
Project management: save/load designs, name projects

sprints.done_when: Full IDE: browse pieces left, 3D design center, AI chat right. Switch to instruction mode and step through assembly. This is the MVP.
